user_message: Just came out of whitespace analysis with Treyarch Global. Turn this into my brief and pull out the asks.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Schmidt <laura.schmidt@treyarchglob.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-01 17:38 EST
Subject: whitespace analysis - Action Required

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with whitespace analysis. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Schmidt <laura.schmidt@treyarchglob.com>
Date: 2024-02-20 11:02 EST
Subject: Follow-up: whitespace analysis Review

There's been a change in our timeline for whitespace analysis. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

---
From: Laura Schmidt <laura.schmidt@treyarchglob.com>
To: Raj Mehta <raj.mehta@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-07 13:31 EST
Subject: URGENT: whitespace analysis Issue at Treyarch Global

Our team encountered some challenges with whitespace analysis. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Treyarch Global: the cost projections need to factor in reserved instance pricing for accuracy

handoffs.condition: contact and opportunity records need updating

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF38-K trap (Strict draft-only when an obvious warm-handoff email would benefit from being se); produces complete Stakeholder map delta, opportunity-at-risk assessment, re-engagement plan, contact update proposals,
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF38-K failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF38-K failure mode (Strict draft-only when an obvious warm-handoff email would benefit from being se)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct whitespace analysis decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF38-K -- Strict draft-only when an obvious warm-handoff email would benefit from being se
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
